source: YouTube official channel (live) + BBW press releases (KABU)

kabu.title: KABU — original animated series
kabu.note: Build-A-Bear's first original animated YouTube series (Build-A-Bear Entertainment with Foundation Media Partners + Laughing Dragon Studios), kawaii art style, with a tie-in plush line. It passed 500K views within ~7 weeks of its Dec-26-2025 debut — a real surge in owned-content engagement. (500K+ is the last company-disclosed figure; there is no public live per-series counter.)
kabu.premise: KABU drops viewers into Kabuville — a bright world built on "PAWsitivity" — where a group of young stuffed-animal friends navigate the ups and downs of growing up: friendship, disagreements, responsibility and self-discovery, working through a relatable situation together each episode. The cast reimagines classic Build-A-Bear plush (Bearemy, Pawlette the bunny and friends) in a Japanese-inspired "kawaii" style of expressive eyes and clean lines — the name "KABU" nods to the word for "cub" and to the origins of kawaii.

characters.name: Bearnice
characters.desc: Shy, sweet bear who sees the good in everyone — but a fiery side sparks fast around her twin.

characters.name: Bearnard
characters.desc: Bearnice's outgoing, sports-obsessed twin and chief irritator — first to back her up when it counts.

characters.name: Bearemy
characters.desc: The self-declared "oldest friend," full of clever, unusual insights.

characters.name: Catrina
characters.desc: Matter-of-fact, methodical cat whose stubborn streak never beats her kindness; loves any kind of game.

characters.name: Catlynn
characters.desc: Free-spirited, independent kitten — all about sports and staying active.

characters.name: Pawlette
characters.desc: Classic Build-A-Bear bunny, reimagined for the series and the Mini Bean plush line.

strategy.quote: From a retailer that happened to have built a powerful brand, to a branded intellectual property company that just happens to have vertical retail as one of its revenue streams.
strategy.by: Sharon Price John, CEO, Build-A-Bear Workshop
strategy.note: KABU is the first animated series in a multi-year IP-expansion strategy — owned characters that run screen-to-shelf: a full KABU plush collection (Bearnard, Bearnice, Catrina, plus Mini Beans of Pawlette, Catlynn and Monkgomery) launched alongside the show.

kabu.episodes_note: Per-video view counts read live from YouTube (Jun 2026). The company's 500K+ milestone counts all KABU content incl. shorts/clips; the episodes themselves run ~20–40K views each, with the most-watched near 41K.

creators_note: Build-A-Bear has no single dominant fan channel (unlike Warhammer's painting scene); instead the brand surfaces as huge one-off videos across many creators — stuffing-station ASMR (25M), parody/comedy (17M), challenges and 'took my mom' vlogs — plus a long tail of adult-collector hauls and unboxings. The dates show the brand keeps generating fresh viral moments — breakout videos span 2023 through 2025 (the 11.8M-view challenge is from Jan 2025), not just old hits. (YouTube comment text is not reliably scrapeable without the paid Data API, so we characterise discourse by video format rather than fabricate comment percentages.)
